check: Meta Pixel and Conversions API (CAPI) both implemented, with event deduplication via event_id
description: Both browser-side Pixel and server-side CAPI fire for the same events, deduplicated via a shared event_id so the same conversion isn't double-counted
why_it_matters: This is the direct Meta-specific application of `ads-server-side-tracking` principles — CAPI recovers signal lost to ad blockers and ITP/cookie restrictions, but only pays off if deduplication is correctly implemented (otherwise it inflates reported conversions)

check: Event Match Quality (EMQ) score reviewed and optimized, not ignored
description: Meta's Event Match Quality diagnostic (found in Events Manager) is checked periodically, and match-quality-improving fields (hashed email, phone, external_id) are added where missing
why_it_matters: Higher EMQ directly improves Meta's ability to attribute conversions to the right user, which improves both optimization quality and reported accuracy — an ignored low EMQ score silently degrades performance

check: Standard events used correctly (Purchase, Lead, CompleteRegistration) matching actual funnel stages
description: Standard Meta events are mapped to their actual corresponding business action, not misused (e.g. firing Purchase on a page view, or never firing a genuine Lead event for a lead-gen business)
why_it_matters: Advantage+ and campaign optimization rely on accurate standard event signals — misused events degrade optimization quality the same way inaccurate conversion actions do in Google Ads (`ADS001`)

check: Multiple distinct creative concepts tested per ad set, not near-duplicate variations
description: Ad creative variations within a campaign represent genuinely different concepts/angles (not just color or headline tweaks of the same underlying creative), supporting Andromeda/Entity-ID-era retrieval which rewards genuine diversity
why_it_matters: In the current Andromeda-era delivery system, creative diversity itself is a signal the system uses for retrieval — near-duplicate variations don't provide the diversity the algorithm can meaningfully use

check: Creative refreshed before fatigue sets in, monitored via frequency and CTR decline
description: Ad creative is monitored for fatigue signals (rising frequency, declining CTR/CPA over time within a stable audience) and refreshed proactively rather than left running until performance visibly collapses
why_it_matters: Creative fatigue is one of the most common, preventable causes of gradually declining Meta ad performance — catching it via leading indicators (frequency, CTR trend) is cheaper than reacting after CPA has already spiked

check: Platform-native creative formats used (vertical video for Reels/Stories, not repurposed horizontal video)
description: Creative assets are produced or adapted specifically for the placement/format they'll run in (vertical 9:16 for Reels/Stories, square/vertical for Feed) rather than a single horizontal video reused everywhere
why_it_matters: Platform-native-feeling content consistently outperforms visibly repurposed content across Meta's short-form video placements — this is both a creative-quality and a technical-spec issue

check: Advantage+ Shopping/App campaigns given accurate creative and audience signal inputs, not launched with minimal setup
description: Advantage+ automated campaign types are launched with genuine creative diversity and accurate catalog/conversion data, since these campaigns lean heavily on automation and need good inputs to optimize well
why_it_matters: Advantage+ campaigns are 'creative-as-targeting' by design — the automation's targeting quality is bottlenecked by creative input quality far more than in traditional manual-targeting campaigns

check: Campaign objective (Sales/Leads/App) matches actual business goal, not defaulted to whatever was pre-selected
description: The campaign objective selected at creation genuinely matches the business's actual conversion goal, reviewed deliberately rather than left at a default that happened to be pre-selected in the campaign creation flow
why_it_matters: Objective selection determines what Meta's delivery system optimizes toward at a fundamental level — a mismatched objective (e.g. Traffic instead of Sales for an e-commerce account) optimizes toward the wrong outcome entirely regardless of how well everything else is configured

check: Consolidated campaign/ad set structure (fewer, larger ad sets) rather than heavy fragmentation
description: Ad sets are structured to concentrate conversion volume (fewer, larger ad sets) rather than heavily fragmented by narrow audience/placement combinations, mirroring the Smart-Bidding-era Google Ads guidance (`ADS005`)
why_it_matters: Meta's delivery system, like Google's, needs sufficient conversion volume per ad set to exit the learning phase and optimize effectively — fragmentation starves each ad set of the data needed for good delivery

check: Learning phase respected — major changes not made repeatedly before an ad set exits it
description: Significant edits to an ad set (budget, audience, creative) are avoided during the learning phase (Meta's own diagnostic flags this) since each significant edit resets learning
why_it_matters: This mirrors `ADS013`'s Google Ads guidance — repeatedly resetting the learning phase produces consistently mediocre, never-optimized delivery

check: Broad or Advantage+ audience targeting used as the default, narrow manual targeting reserved for specific tested reasons
description: Audience targeting defaults to broad/Advantage+ audiences (Meta's current recommended default, leaning on creative and signal quality to find the right users) rather than heavily narrowed manual interest/demographic targeting by default
why_it_matters: Meta's delivery system has consistently shown broad targeting outperforming narrow manual targeting when creative and conversion signal quality are strong — narrow targeting artificially constrains the algorithm's ability to find the best-converting users

check: Retargeting audiences excluded from prospecting campaigns to avoid budget overlap
description: Custom audiences of recent converters/high-intent visitors are excluded from broad prospecting campaigns so budget isn't spent re-targeting people already captured by a dedicated retargeting campaign
why_it_matters: Overlap between prospecting and retargeting campaigns without exclusions means both campaigns compete for the same users, muddying attribution and potentially wasting prospecting budget on already-converted users

check: Ad copy and creative comply with Meta's advertising policies (health, financial, housing/employment restrictions)
description: Ads in regulated categories (health claims, financial services, housing, employment, credit) follow Meta's specific policy restrictions for that category, including any required Special Ad Category settings
why_it_matters: Policy violations in these categories range from ad rejection to account-level restrictions — Special Ad Category housing/employment/credit ads specifically have targeting restrictions (no age/gender/zip targeting) that must be configured correctly, not just avoided in copy

check: Bid strategy and budget align with actual conversion volume needed to exit learning phase
description: Daily budget is set high enough relative to target CPA/ROAS that the ad set can realistically generate the ~50 conversions/week commonly cited as needed to exit the learning phase
why_it_matters: An underfunded ad set relative to its CPA target can spend indefinitely without ever generating enough conversion volume to optimize properly — this is a root-cause diagnosis worth checking before concluding an audience/creative isn't working
